d e 
Refactoring framework.
Used as a main program, this can refactor any number of files and/or
recursively descend down directories.  Imported as a module, this
provides infrastructure to write your own refactoring tool.
z#Guido van Rossum <guido@python.org>
chain
driver
tokenize
token)

Inspects the options to load the requested patterns and handlers.
        Returns:
          (pre_order, post_order), where pre_order is the list of fixers that
          want a pre-order AST traversal, and post_order is the list that want
          post-order traversal.

refactor
RefactoringTool.refactorc
	|	|
Descends down a directory and refactor every Python file found.
        Python files are assumed to have a .py extension.
        Files and subdirectories starting with '.' are skipped.
        
Descending into %sr2
z0RefactoringTool.refactor_dir.<locals>.<listcomp>N)

RefactoringTool.refactor_filec
Refactor a given input string.
        Args:
            data: a string holding the code to be refactored.
            name: a human-readable name for use in error/log messages.
        Returns:
            An AST corresponding to the refactored input stream; None if
            there were errors during the parse.
        rZ
Can't parse %s: %s: %sNz
Refactoring %s)
Z!python_grammar_no_print_statementr
parse_stringr"

<stdin>z

No changes in stdin)
stdinr
refactor_stdinv
RefactoringTool.refactor_stdinc
]*}	|	|
qjqTt
Refactors a parse tree (modifying the tree in place).
        For compatible patterns the bottom matcher module is
        used. Otherwise the tree is traversed node-to-node for
        matches.
        Args:
            tree: a pytree.Node instance representing the root of the tree
                  to be refactored.
            name: a human-readable name for this tree.
        Returns:
            True if the tree was modified, False otherwise.
        T)
reverser|
start_tree
traverse_byrs

	transform

Z	match_set
node
results
newZ
new_matchesZ
fxrr
RefactoringTool.refactor_treec
Traverse an AST, applying a set of fixers to each node.
        This is a helper method for refactor_tree().
        Args:
            fixers: a list of fixer instances.
            traversal: a generator that yields AST nodes.
        Returns:
            None
        N)
Z	traversalr
RefactoringTool.traverse_byc
        Called when a file has been refactored and there may be changes.
        Nr
No changes to %sz
Not writing changes to %s)
write_file)
RefactoringTool.processed_filec
Writes a string to a file.
        It first shows a unified diff between the old text and the new text, and
        then rewrites the file; the latter is only done if the write option is
        set.
        
Can't create %s: %sNz
Can't write %s: %sz
Wrote changes to %sT)
RefactoringTool.write_filez
>>> z
... c
Refactors a docstring, looking for doctests.
        This returns a modified version of the input string.  It looks
        for doctests, which start with a ">>>" prompt, and may be
        continued with "..." prompts, as long as the "..." is indented
        the same as the ">>>".
        (Unfortunately we can't use the doctest module's parser,
        since, like most parsers, it is not geared towards preserving
        the original source.)
        Nr
keependsr
splitlines
lstripr
PS1r-
refactor_doctest
find
rstripr
result
blockZ
block_lineno
indent
lineno
line
z"RefactoringTool.refactor_docstringc
Refactors one doctest.
        A doctest is given as a block of lines, the first of which starts
        with ">>>" (possibly indented), while the remaining lines start
        with "..." (identically indented).
        z
Source: %sr
z+Can't parse docstring in %s line %s: %s: %sNTr

RefactoringTool.summarizec
Parses a block into a tree.
        This is necessary to get correct line number / offset information
        in the parser diagnostics and embedded into the parse tree.
        )

RefactoringTool.wrap_toksc
Generates lines as expected by tokenize from a list of lines.
        This strips the first len(indent + self.PS1) characters off each line.
        Nr
